mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below 1%) reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lowers the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11 in snapshot indicating near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS < 1% are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10), or rank IC of FEDFUNDS level with forward returns stays below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and forthcoming earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days once the rise exceeds a material threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over the trailing 3 months
failure_condition: When the 0.3pp/3mo UNRATE rise trigger fires, subsequent 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or rank IC conditional on trigger is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand weakness that is not evident from either series alone; this joint deceleration predicts a slower-building negative drift in S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days) as earnings revisions catch down to weak demand signals.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth negative AND HOUST month-over-month growth negative concurrently for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 439771 and HOUST at 1089.0 in snapshot
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST contraction periods show 60-day forward SPX returns not significantly below the unconditional 60-day mean (p > 0.10), or the interaction term fails to improve rank IC over either single-series model by more than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During an expansionary phase, positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) confirms real economic momentum that supports near-term risk appetite, producing modest positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; the effect is absent or reversed outside expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.5114 and GDPC1 at 16311.6 in snapshot
regime_hint: expansionary phase with positive real GDP growth
failure_condition: During periods classified as expansion (positive trailing GDPC1 growth), positive INDPRO momentum fails to produce 5-day forward returns above the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.10) in more than one 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive trend growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects durable labor income growth that supports consumption and corporate revenues, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 6-month PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth periods do not outperform negative-growth periods with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation crosses above a level that raises expectations of policy tightening, forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days turn negative as investors price in higher discount rates and margin compression risk.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY > 4% trigger is active, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales growth (RSAFS) signals a synchronized, self-reinforcing demand expansion that is more predictive of sustained equity gains over the next quarter (60 trading days) than either series individually, because it confirms both aggregate output and consumer-level spending are strengthening together.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS trailing 3-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16311.6 and RSAFS at 439771 in snapshot
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1+RSAFS acceleration periods produce 60-day forward returns not significantly above baseline (p > 0.10), or the interaction model's rank IC does not exceed the best single-series model by at least 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime where unemployment is near historic lows and the Federal Reserve is actively raising rates, the combination signals labor market overheating that the central bank is trying to cool, increasing the probability of a near-term growth scare and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price rising recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.7 and FEDFUNDS at 0.11 in snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with low unemployment
failure_condition: In periods jointly characterized by UNRATE below its trailing 5-year median and FEDFUNDS rising over the trailing 6 months, 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.10), rejecting the regime-conditional effect.
